plan_binding_snapshot.title: CP00-288 RP08.P06.M00.S07-RP08.P06.M07.S06 generated_from_weighted_implementation_ledger
plan_binding_snapshot.renumbering_policy: Pack IDs are monotonic after the latest live closeout pack. If a requested locked queue skips live-open ledger units, live-open units are inserted first and requested packs are shifted with correction_reason.

implementation_summary.summary: CP00-288 implements descriptor-only coverage of the RP08.P06 permission body (M00 scope inventory tail, M01 contract draft, M02-M06 full permission cycles with cross-tenant and leak-prevention tests, M07 test/golden head) across eight sections over 150 planned rows covering permission matrix rows, six decision bindings, audit hint fields, matched rule captures, deny-over-allow checks, legal hold/ethical wall/object ACL interactions, review/approval routes, security trimming proofs, audit event expectations, permission fixtures, and allowed/denied tests, with Hermes evidence (H08), Claude review packet (C08), and CP00-289 handoff, without opening runtime permission evaluation, audit event writes, AI runtime dispatch, state writes, object storage, command runtime execution, Hermes runtime receipts, Citation Ledger, or Loop behavior.

parsed_review.summary: CP00-288 is a descriptor-only email/DMS permission slice (RP08.P06.M00.S07-RP08.P06.M07.S06, risk C, 150 units) whose pack-level readiness claim is supported by independent read-only inspection. The live closeout-pack-plan, manifest plan_binding_snapshot, contract projection, registry binding, service case-set/descriptor, validators, test suite, and contract validator are mutually consistent and free of drift: the CP288 binding matches the plan and baseline exactly (upstream CP00-287, next CP00-289 at RP08.P06.M07.S07, ledger SHA 13c7ed65), and deliverable/micro distributions equal the baseline (impl 56 / security_audit 35 / ui 28 / claude_review 7 / test 24 = 150). The validators genuinely enforce the claimed boundaries rather than asserting them cosmetically: freezeCp288Result plus CP288/CP287 row-extras set the exact fields validateEmailDmsCoreCp288PermissionSliceDescriptor checks (descriptor_only, no runtime_execution, deny_over_allow_enforced, permission_decision_detail_included:false, writes_audit_event:false, real_client_data_loaded:false, cross_tenant_access_allowed:false, leak_detected:false), and validate-rp08-email-dms-core-contract.mjs deep-equals the committed contract against code constants. No-write/no-real-data boundaries hold (command-evidence no_real_data:true, writes_product_state:false; manifest implementation_summary affirms no runtime permission/audit/AI/object-storage/Hermes-receipt execution). Authority boundary is correct: claude_is_final_approval:false, enterprise trust not claimed from local validation, human final approval required, Hermes (H08) used only for deterministic descriptor evidence packets with no product runtime receipt. production_ready:false / status implemented_pending_review is appropriately NOT over-claimed; the absent post-review artifacts (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) reflect correct pre-review sequencing (pack_level_claude_review.status:pending). Review-hardening evidence is sound: readiness auth-preflight passed (exit 0, 1454-byte valid JSON result), no CP00-288 invalid attempts recorded, and the unrelated LDIP invalid-attempt file is correctly scoped out. One P3 cosmetic metadata inconsistency in changed-file-scope.json with no gate impact. No P0/P1/P2 issues; pack and goal closeout are not blocked.

findings.title: changed-file-scope.json dual-classifies pack evidence dir and handoff doc as both active-untracked and unrelated-preserved
findings.details: docs/closeout-packs/cp00-288/ and docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md appear in both untracked_implementation_files (lines 95-98) and unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (lines 100-105). These are contradictory labels: a file cannot be simultaneously an active untracked implementation file for this pack and an unrelated preserved file. The manifest's worktree_transition_policy.unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (manifest.json lines 5985-5988) uses the narrower, correct set (only the stray .docx and the unrelated LDIP invalid-attempt JSON), so the canonical scoping is unaffected. The dual-listing is confined to generated reviewer-scope metadata covering documentation/evidence files; it does not touch any product runtime artifact and has no effect on the production_ready determination, validation gates, or no-write/no-real-data boundaries.
findings.recommendation: Align changed-file-scope.json so the pack's own evidence directory (docs/closeout-packs/cp00-288/) and handoff doc are listed only as active pack artifacts, not under unrelated_dirty_files_preserved, matching the manifest's worktree_transition_policy classification. Cosmetic; safe to defer.

commit_policy.message: Close CP00-288 email dms core permission slice descriptor pack
